passage.title: Front Desk Mail Table
passage.text: Our group keeps a return card board next to the garden gate. In the afternoon, Joon reads it before our walk home. The return card board has name cards and short notes for each class for the day's job. The desk helper asks us to help the small garden in order. Sometimes we compare today's note with yesterday's note and notice one small change. We like the return card board because it helps us deliver things correctly.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the return card board is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the return card board?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the return card board is next to the garden gate.

questions.prompt: When does Joon use the return card board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Joon uses it in the afternoon.

questions.prompt: What does the return card board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the return card board has name cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
